nextFocus: Take Batch 4 next: tighten CI, Docker, install/release paths, and cross-platform docs so the release surface reflects the real runtime maturity.
Keep reducing unverified claims while adding the next runtime-complete slices only where they can be built and tested honestly.
remainingGaps: The project still lacks a finished agentsh-style installable drop-in shell replacement and broader end-to-end shim deployment story.
Platform-native enforcement remains partial: Linux and macOS are still largely planning surfaces, Windows containment is still limited, and network blocking is not yet true OS-level interception.
Cross-platform packaging, Docker validation, and release automation are still behind the remaining product claims.
strengths: The repository now enforces command, network, and explicit environment override policies through the live execution path with automated regression coverage.
Daemon usability for supported non-interactive flows improved materially in Batch 2, and Batch 3 removed one more dead-config gap by turning env_rules into runtime behavior.
The tested CLI and daemon surface is increasingly honest: docs and current behavior now align more closely for start/stop/status/session and policy handling.
